steps: Go to https://account.mapbox.com/auth/signup/ and create a free account, or log in at https://account.mapbox.com
Once logged in, navigate to https://account.mapbox.com/access-tokens/
Enter a descriptive name for the token
Select the required scopes for your use case (e.g., styles:read, geocoding:read, etc.)
Optionally add URL restrictions to limit where the token can be used
Click 'Create token' and confirm with your account password
Copy the generated access token — it is shown in the token list and can be retrieved later

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Access Token
credential_fields.description: Your Mapbox access token. Passed as the 'access_token' query parameter or as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

notes: Tokens starting with 'pk.' are public tokens; tokens starting with 'sk.' are secret tokens with write access
The default public token in your account can be used immediately for most read operations
Access tokens can be retrieved from the dashboard at any time (unlike some other services)
Free tier includes 50,000 map loads/month and generous API call limits across geocoding, directions, etc.
Tokens can be scoped to specific API scopes and restricted to specific URLs for security
The 'access_token' query parameter and Authorization Bearer header are both supported

test_endpoint.description: Forward geocode 'Paris' to verify the access token works (append ?access_token=YOUR_TOKEN to the URL)
